title: Lineara candidate-signature hypothesis (v1)
description: Multi-root hypothesis (mg-bef2, harness v9). A *signature* proposes that a window of an inscription jointly admits a SET of substrate roots that together cover most of the syllabograms in the window. The union of all roots' sign_to_phoneme entries forms a single non-conflicting partial sign->phoneme mapping; downstream metrics that consume a partial mapping (notably external_phoneme_perplexity_v0) score that union directly. Distinct from candidate_equation.v1 (one root per equation), this shape preserves the co-occurrence structure of real lexicons (kinship + kinship, accountancy + accountancy).

schema_version.description: Discriminator. The harness loader dispatches on this field.

name.description: Stable slug; should match the file basename. Mixed case permitted so inscription IDs (HT, ZA, KH, ...) read naturally.

created.description: ISO-8601 date the hypothesis was authored.

source_pool.description: Substrate pool tag the roots are drawn from. Used by the sweep runner to dispatch the external language model.

span.description: Inclusive [start, end] over the inscription's tokens[] array.

roots.description: Ordered list of root placements within the window. Spans must be non-overlapping; sign_to_phoneme entries across roots must not assign a single sign to two different phonemes.

sign_to_phoneme.description: Sign -> phoneme map for the syllabograms covered by THIS root. Keys are corpus sign tokens that fall within the root's span; one entry per distinct sign. The same sign may appear at multiple positions within the root if it maps to the same phoneme everywhere (e.g. ama: AB05 -> a, AB10 -> m, AB05 -> a collapses to {AB05: a, AB10: m}).

span_within_window.description: Inclusive [start, end] offsets relative to window.span[0]. Must be within the window and not overlap any other root's span.

coverage.description: Coverage statistics persisted at generation time. Recomputed and validated against the active corpus when the hypothesis is loaded.
